headline: Bun Consolidates the JavaScript Runtime and Build Toolchain
lede: Bun replaces fragmented dependency trees with a unified, high-performance runtime built on JavaScriptCore. By integrating test running and bundling into a single executable, it offers an efficient migration path for modern full-stack frameworks.

headline: A Curated Library of Tools for Research-Grade AI Agents
lede: This collection provides 135 standardized modules, enabling AI agents to interact directly with bioinformatics, physics, and clinical databases. It serves as an essential reference for researchers bridging the gap between large language models and specialized computational workflows.

headline: Superpowers Enforces Disciplined Engineering for AI Coding Assistants
lede: This framework moves beyond reactive code generation by implementing mandatory planning and self-review protocols. Its plugin-based architecture ensures that agents maintain project context and adhere to strict test-driven development standards throughout the build cycle.

headline: Open Generative AI Offers Filter-Free Media Creation
lede: With a dual-engine architecture supporting both local hardware and dedicated GPU servers, this studio provides a transparent interface for image and video synthesis. It eliminates vendor-imposed guardrails, giving creators total sovereignty over their generative pipelines.

headline: Supertonic Delivers High-Performance Text-to-Speech Without Cloud Dependencies
lede: Built on the ONNX Runtime, Supertonic scales from edge devices to desktops while keeping models under 100 million parameters. This benchmark-focused implementation proves that high-quality speech synthesis can be achieved locally without network latency.

headline: OpenHuman Secures Contextual Data in Local Markdown Vaults
lede: OpenHuman reclaims fragmented personal data from SaaS silos by synchronizing it into a private, encrypted Obsidian-compatible archive. Its memory-tree architecture ensures that AI assistants work exclusively with on-device context, safeguarding sensitive user information.

headline: RuView Reconstructs Spatial Data From Standard WiFi Signals
lede: By analyzing channel state information, this system maps human presence and vital signs using nothing but radio scatter patterns. The pipeline converts low-cost hardware inputs into real-time, high-fidelity spatial intelligence without the need for intrusive cameras.

headline: Witr Visualizes Process Lineage and Dependency Chains
lede: When standard process monitors fall short, Witr maps the causality of active tasks across containers and shells. This CLI utility strips away the noise, providing a direct view of why a service exists and how it was initiated.

headline: CodeGraph Optimizes Agentic Context With Local Semantic Mapping
lede: CodeGraph replaces latency-heavy file scanning with an SQLite-backed semantic index of code relationships. It provides a raw, performant foundation for AI agents to query call graphs and architectural structures in real time.

headline: Rapid Adoption of Local-First AI Workflows
lede: The shift toward local-first infrastructure continues to accelerate as developers prioritize data sovereignty and performance. These tools collectively signal a departure from centralized, cloud-dependent architectures toward robust, self-contained computing environments.
